people_highlights.name: Yann LeCun
people_highlights.post:
Dario is wrong.
He knows absolutely nothing about the effects of technological revolutions on the labor market. 
Don't listen to him, Sam, Yoshua, Geoff, or me on this topic.
Listen to economists who have spent their career studying this, like @Ph_Aghion , @erikbryn ,

people_highlights.name: Simon Willison
people_highlights.post:
Is there still a widespread belief that LLMs and coding agents are good for greenfield development but don't help for maintaining large existing codebases?

I don't think that idea holds up any more
people_highlights.why: Recent post from AI commentator at Independent

people_highlights.name: François Chollet
people_highlights.post: Constraints are the catalyst of invention. An infinite search space leads to paralysis. The most creative inventions happen when you are forced to solve a problem within appropriately narrow constraints.
people_highlights.why: Recent post from Keras creator at Google

people_highlights.name: Ethan Mollick
people_highlights.post:
An obvious way to release Mythos class models with uncertain autonomous ability is to make them only available on the website, like Gemini Deep Think or ChatGPT Pro.

Minimal risk of being used for autonomous hacking, but accessible to people who have hard problems to solve.
